name: engrafo
description: Structural code graph for coding agents. Bi-temporal dependency analysis, symbol search, dead code detection, and change history — without re-reading source files.
author: jomruizgo

mcp_server.description: Starts the engrafo MCP server. Run `engrafo init` first to index your repository.

tools.name: cg_context
tools.description: Returns project-level context: total node count, languages, top symbols by incoming-edge degree, and per-kind counts. Use this as the first call in any session to orient the agent.

tools.name: cg_node
tools.description: Returns full details for a single symbol: active outgoing and incoming edges, historical invalidated edges (with valid_until_commit), engram observation anchors, and dead-code classification. The primary tool for understanding a specific symbol.

tools.name: cg_dependents
tools.description: Returns all nodes with active incoming edges to the given file. Answers: what will break if I change this file?

tools.name: cg_dependencies
tools.description: Returns all nodes that the given file depends on via active outgoing edges. Answers: what does this file use?

tools.name: cg_impact
tools.description: Transitive blast-radius analysis up to N hops. Returns the full set of files affected by a change to the target file, with depth information.

tools.name: cg_search
tools.description: FTS5 full-text search over symbol names. Supports SQLite FTS5 match syntax. Returns up to limit results ranked by relevance.

tools.name: cg_anchor
tools.description: Links an engram observation UUID to one or more graph nodes by symbol name. Enables bi-temporal correlation between episodic memory and code structure.

tools.name: cg_deadcode
tools.description: Detects dead-code candidates. Orphans: nodes that never had any incoming edge. Abandoned: nodes that had incoming edges and lost them all. The bi-temporal model makes abandoned detection highly accurate — these were actively used and then dropped.

tools.name: cg_history
tools.description: Returns the chronological edge timeline for a symbol: when dependencies appeared and disappeared per commit, with associated engram observation anchors.

workspace_add.command: engrafo workspace add <name> <path> [--remote URL] [--branch B] [--vcs git|none]
workspace_add.description: Register a root in the workspace (creates engrafo.json if absent) and index it immediately.

workspace_list.description: List registered roots with path, VCS, remote, node count, and last indexed date.

workspace_remove.description: Remove a root from the manifest and delete its nodes/edges from the database (cascading).

init.description: Index the repository from the current working tree. Creates .engrafo/graph.db.

init_from_git.description: Replay the last N git commits to build bi-temporal history. More accurate for dead-code detection.

update.description: Incremental update. Without --root: all roots. With --root: only that root. Detects changes by git diff (vcs=git) or checksum (vcs=none).

serve.description: Start the MCP server on stdio. Used by MCP host configuration.

ui.description: Start the local graph browser at http://localhost:8080. Shows force-directed graph, symbol search, node detail with active/historical edges, and dead-code tab.

status.description: Show index statistics: node count, edge count, last commit, freshness.

doctor.description: Verify installation: check db exists, schema version, FTS5 support.

hooks_install.description: Install Claude Code hooks (SessionStart, PreToolUse Read/Write, PostToolUse) into .claude/settings.json.

install.manual: Download the binary for your platform from https://github.com/Jomruizgo/Engrafo/releases
